resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The local fixture supplies a boundary row for GLP-1 and weight-loss medication-response context, but it does not include a validated local response model, source algorithm, or clinical decision context.
resultRows.sourceLabel: FDA Ozempic label; FDA pharmacogenetic resources; MedlinePlus pharmacogenetic tests
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: This local run can say what evidence is present or missing. It cannot say whether Ozempic, Wegovy, Mounjaro, Zepbound, Saxenda, Xenical, Alli, Qsymia, Contrave, Adipex-P, Lomaira, or Imcivree is right for the user.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: Medication decisions need diagnosis, indication, labs, medication history, contraindications, side-effect history, pregnancy context when relevant, and qualified clinician review. Those inputs are not supplied in this synthetic fixture.
resultRows.sourceLabel: FDA Ozempic label; MedlinePlus pharmacogenetic tests
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: The report should stay educational. It should not make start, stop, switch, dose, safety, expected weight-loss, diabetes-outcome, or side-effect claims from genotype alone.

resultRows.item: Golden Gate Gene Ozempic and Other GLP-1 Agonists sample report
resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The public sample PDF shows report identity, table of contents, limitations, an individual response summary, gene-profile chart, drug-specific pharmacogenetic sections, and references. Those rows are preserved below as sampleRows[] only.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: The sample rows show how the original report is structured. They are not the user's medication-response results and should not be used as medication advice.

sampleRows.geneticAnalysis: The public sample PDF cover identifies Ozempic and Other GLP-1 Agonists by Golden Gate Gene and says the report aims to harness genetic data for personalized medical solutions.
sampleRows.description: The table of contents lists Introduction, Summary, Wegovy, Ozempic, Zepbound, Mounjaro, Saxenda, Xenical, Alli, Qsymia, Contrave, Adipex-P, Lomaira, Imcivree, and References.
sampleRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed report identity and table-of-contents structure only; this is not local-user medication-response output.

sampleRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample says it compiles DNA analysis for variants influencing efficacy, safety, metabolism, and receptor affinity of weight-loss medications, while also stating that age, body mass, liver and kidney health, concurrent medications, lifestyle, clinical history, and professional review matter.
sampleRows.description: The sample limitation and disclaimer pages state that the report does not cover every possible variant, non-detection does not rule out altered phenotypes, and the content should not replace professional medical advice, diagnosis, or treatment.
sampleRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed sample limitations and disclaimer only; local output must keep medication decisions with qualified healthcare professionals.

sampleRows.geneticAnalysis: The summary page lists anticipated response to weight-reduction substances and shows a gene-profile chart for CYP2D6, DRD2, CYP3A4, ARRB1, COMT, HTR2C, TCF7L2, GLP1R, GIPR, FASN, CYP3A5, GRIK1, PLXNA4, INSR, ANKK1, TAAR1, POMC, PCSK1, and LEPR, with visible 100% values for most genes and 50% for GRIK1.
sampleRows.description: The sample summary is preserved as report-output structure only. Its response labels and percentages are not local-user medication predictions.
sampleRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed sample summary and gene-profile chart only; not local-user gene activity, drug response, or treatment guidance.

sampleRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample Wegovy/Ozempic section labels the overall semaglutide response as Standard Response, with GLP1R Normal Affinity 100%, ARRB1 Normal Activity 100%, and TCF7L2 Normal Function 100%.
sampleRows.description: Use this as observed section and table shape only; do not infer local semaglutide eligibility, dose, benefit, side effects, diabetes outcome, or weight-loss response.
sampleRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed sample semaglutide section only; not a local Wegovy or Ozempic recommendation.

sampleRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample Zepbound/Mounjaro section labels the overall tirzepatide response as Standard Response, with GLP1R Normal Affinity 100%, GIPR Normal Affinity 100%, and TCF7L2 Normal Function 100%.
sampleRows.description: Use this as observed section and table shape only; do not infer local tirzepatide eligibility, dose, benefit, side effects, diabetes outcome, or weight-loss response.
sampleRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed sample tirzepatide section only; not a local Zepbound or Mounjaro recommendation.

sampleRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample Saxenda section labels the overall liraglutide response as Standard Response, with GLP1R Normal Affinity 100%, ARRB1 Normal Activity 100%, and TCF7L2 Normal Function 100%.
sampleRows.description: Use this as observed section and table shape only; do not infer local liraglutide eligibility, dose, benefit, side effects, diabetes outcome, or weight-loss response.
sampleRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed sample liraglutide section only; not a local Saxenda recommendation.

sampleRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample Xenical/Alli section labels the overall orlistat response as Standard Response, with FASN Normal Activity 100%, CYP3A4 Normal Metabolism 100%, and CYP3A5 Normal Activity 100%.
sampleRows.description: Use this as observed section and table shape only; do not infer local orlistat eligibility, dose, benefit, side effects, or weight-loss response.
sampleRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed sample orlistat section only; not a local Xenical or Alli recommendation.

sampleRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample Qsymia section labels the overall phentermine/topiramate response as Lower Sensitivity, with GRIK1 Decreased Activity 50%, PLXNA4 Normal Activity 100%, and INSR Normal Function 100%.
sampleRows.description: Use this as observed section and table shape only; do not infer local Qsymia eligibility, dose, lower response, benefit, side effects, or weight-loss response.
sampleRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed sample Qsymia section only; not a local phentermine/topiramate response prediction.

sampleRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample Contrave section labels the overall naltrexone/bupropion response as Standard Response, with ANKK1 Normal Affinity 100%, DRD2 Normal Affinity 100%, and INSR Normal Function 100%.
sampleRows.description: Use this as observed section and table shape only; do not infer local Contrave eligibility, dose, benefit, side effects, cravings, mood response, or weight-loss response.
sampleRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed sample Contrave section only; not a local naltrexone/bupropion recommendation.

sampleRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample Adipex-P/Lomaira section labels the overall phentermine response as Standard Response, with TAAR1 Normal Function 100%, COMT Normal Activity 100%, and HTR2C Normal Affinity 100%.
sampleRows.description: Use this as observed section and table shape only; do not infer local phentermine eligibility, dose, benefit, side effects, mood effect, or weight-loss response.
sampleRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed sample phentermine section only; not a local Adipex-P or Lomaira recommendation.

sampleRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample Imcivree section labels the overall setmelanotide response as Standard Response, with POMC Normal Function 100%, PCSK1 Normal Function 100%, and LEPR Normal Function 100%.
sampleRows.description: Use this as observed section and table shape only; do not infer local setmelanotide eligibility, dose, rare-obesity diagnosis, benefit, side effects, or weight-loss response.
sampleRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed sample setmelanotide section only; not a local Imcivree recommendation or rare-obesity diagnosis.

sampleRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample reference pages list 18 sources, including GLP-1 receptor agonist pharmacogenomics, liraglutide pharmacogenomic trial work, GIP receptor variant signaling, orlistat/FASN context, CYP3A activity, topiramate/pharmacogenetic commentary, phentermine/topiramate case context, antiobesity medication pharmacogenomics, DRD2 naltrexone/bupropion work, TAAR1 background, and setmelanotide/LiverTox context.
sampleRows.description: The local package preserves citation-list structure and still uses curated FDA, pharmacogenetic-test, and public-health references for safety boundaries.
sampleRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed sample reference-list structure only; individual external articles are not treated as local response calibration unless separately supplied.

drugDecisionContext.plainEnglishMeaning: This package can reproduce the formal shape of the Ozempic/GLP-1 sample report and disclose what local evidence is unavailable. It cannot decide medication eligibility, dose, drug choice, expected response, side effects, or treatment action.
unsupportedClaims: Ozempic, Wegovy, Mounjaro, Zepbound, Saxenda, Xenical, Alli, Qsymia, Contrave, Adipex-P, Lomaira, or Imcivree drug choice
dose, start, stop, or switch guidance
drugDecisionContext.professionalReview: Personal medication decisions require validated test scope, source-output provenance, clinical context, medication history, contraindication review, and qualified healthcare professional oversight.

genotypeSummary.effect: The public sample summary and drug sections show these genes in semaglutide, tirzepatide, liraglutide, and glycemic-response context.
genotypeSummary.phenotype: Observed sample chart values only; do not infer local GLP-1 or GIP receptor activity, diabetes outcome, weight-loss response, or medication choice.

genotypeSummary.effect: The sample Wegovy/Ozempic and Saxenda sections show ARRB1 Normal Activity 100% in GLP-1 receptor desensitization/internalization context.
genotypeSummary.phenotype: Observed sample chart value only; do not infer local semaglutide or liraglutide response.

genotypeSummary.effect: The sample Xenical/Alli section shows these genes with normal activity or metabolism values in orlistat response context.
genotypeSummary.phenotype: Observed sample chart values only; do not infer local orlistat response, side effects, or medication choice.

genotypeSummary.effect: The sample Qsymia section shows GRIK1 Decreased Activity 50%, PLXNA4 Normal Activity 100%, and INSR Normal Function 100%.
genotypeSummary.phenotype: Observed sample chart values only; do not infer local phentermine/topiramate response, appetite suppression, glycemic effect, or medication choice.

genotypeSummary.effect: The sample Contrave section shows normal affinity/function values for reward-processing and insulin-signaling genes.
genotypeSummary.phenotype: Observed sample chart values only; do not infer local naltrexone/bupropion response, cravings, mood, or medication choice.

genotypeSummary.effect: The sample Adipex-P/Lomaira section shows these genes with normal function, activity, or affinity values in phentermine context.
genotypeSummary.phenotype: Observed sample chart values only; do not infer local phentermine response, mood effect, side effects, or medication choice.

genotypeSummary.effect: The sample Imcivree section shows these genes with normal function values in setmelanotide and melanocortin-pathway context.
genotypeSummary.phenotype: Observed sample chart values only; do not infer local rare-obesity diagnosis, setmelanotide eligibility, response, or medication choice.
